channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Nintendo Promises Strong Switch 2 Lineup After Price Increase
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo’s president, Shuntaro Furukawa, recently commented on the price hike for the Nintendo Switch 2 and his hopes to address the issue by bolstering the platform’s game lineup. During its latest financial earnings release, Nintendo revealed that it intends to raise the price of the Switch 2. Starting in September, Switch 2 consoles will cost an extra $50 in the United States. Markets in Asia are also being hit with an increase in the Nintendo Switch Online membership prices.

Nintendo executives are once again apologizing for the inconvenience that this may cause consumers. However, they also say they plan to load up the Switch 2 lineup to give players more value.

Nintendo Hopes New Switch 2 Games Offset Rising Console Costs

During a recent investor call, Nintendo president Shuntaro Furukawa announced that the company will prepare a “robust” software lineup to overcome the pricing barrier. However, regarding the pricing barrier, he also stated that the new pricing doesn’t fully account for all cost increases.

“We sincerely apologize to our customers for the considerable inconvenience and trouble this will cause,” he said (as translated by Nintendo Patents Watch on Bluesky Social). “While we wanted to prioritize a wide adoption, it was challenging to bear the rising costs over a long period. The [new] pricing does not fully account for all cost increases.”

So what could be coming as part of the robust lineup? Well, there is a “new” Star Fox game on the way. It is an updated take on Star Fox 64, however. Players are patiently waiting for the expected Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake. Also, there has been no official word from Nintendo on when the next 3-D Mario will be released. Prominent leaker NateTheHate recently stated that gamers shouldn’t expect that title until 2027. Speaking of 2027, that is when the generation 10 Pokémon games, pokémon wind and Pokémon Waves, will be released.

Already released are strong games in the Donkey Kong series and Pokémon Pokopia. Pokopia has sold millions of copies so far. Third-party games such as Resident Evil Requiem are also bolstering the console’s lineup.

For more information on the planned price increases, click here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

What do you hope to see come to the Nintendo Switch 2 in the near future?

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: WWE Sponsorship Could Hint at Gears of War: E-Day Launch
item.content:encoded:
Gears of War: E-Day is sponsoring a major professional wrestling event in September. While nothing is certain, this could provide a hint at the game’s release window.

During WWE Backlash on May 9, it was announced that AAA Triplemania, the biggest show of the year for WWE’s Lucha Libre promotion in Mexico, would be taking place on September 11 and September 13. Additionally, it was announced that Gears of War: E-Day would be a presenting sponsor of the events. Again, this isn’t a definitive sign that the game will be available by the time the event goes on the air in September, but in the past, WWE would only have video game sponsors for events releasing around the time of the event.

This is also not the first time WWE has partnered up with Gears of War. Kofi Kingston, Big E Langston, and Xavier Woods, collectively known as The New Day, were playable characters in Gears of War 5. WWE has also partnered with Fortnite and other video games in the past.

TRIPLEMANÍA por primera ocasión será un evento de DOS NOCHES 💥 11 y 13 de septiembre de 2026 Para más información regístrate en wwe.com/triplemania34

While there is no definitive release date for the next Gears of War game, a presentation focused on it is scheduled for June. Since 2024, no significant gameplay footage has been revealed. The game was initially announced at the 2024 Xbox Showcase in June, where only a cinematic trailer was shown. The upcoming presentation will be held on June 7, immediately following an Xbox showcase. It was recently revealed that the event would present about 30 minutes of new footage.

“I’ll say that we’ve got the Gears Direct coming, and after seeing that, we’ve probably got like a full 30 minutes, which is really great. I don’t know. I just love everybody in that studio, the work they’re doing. I’m excited about the game, and we’re going to have a fantastic, full 30 minutes of stuff to see,” Xbox CCO Matt Booty proclaimed in a recent interview.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. For more WWE video game-related news, check out the locker codes for the month of May in WWE 2K26.

Are you excited for Gears of War: E-Day? Do you think there will be more crossovers between WWE and the Gears of War franchise? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
